paragraphs: I make paper cut-outs, drawings, and paintings that mimic organic forms. My pattern series began in childhood as a source of comfort. Its design stems from nature such as trees and flowers.

I create patterns that gradually consume everything, including themselves. Layered webs reach across, covering what lies beneath such as archaeological sites and emotional states expressed through color, mark-making, texture, sewing, and composition. The pattern investigates, frames, and obscures, similar to how the external world shapes us and continues after us.

My work is also informed by the Pattern and Decoration Movement. Joyce Kozloff noted that work described as “decorative” was labeled feminine and therefore inferior because of its connection to women and marginalized cultures. I use patterns to challenge patriarchal and capitalist systems by avoiding their artistic language.
